serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 320 ml aluminium can of Coca-Cola Original Taste, as sold in Singapore retail and food-service outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Coca-Cola Original Taste Can 320ml — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100 g taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Coca-Cola Original Taste Can 320ml (cr_id G00010030). Saturated fat and trans fat values are confirmed 0 g by product formulation and consistent with product label data. HPB Foodo is an approved primary regional source for Singapore dishes and packaged products.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of cola-type carbonated soft drinks — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for Coca-Cola is directly listed in the University of Sydney GI Database and confirmed in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 63 × 33.15 g / 100 = 20.9. Carbohydrate per 320 g serving = 10.36 g/100 g × 3.20 = 33.152 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.94 g sugar per 100 g; high fructose corn syrup drives insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain independent of dietary sodium content
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Coca-Cola and other sugar-sweetened beverages entirely, as regular consumption of beverages containing >5 g sugar per 100 g is independently associated with elevated blood pressure via fructose-driven uric acid production and sympathetic nervous system activation (AHA 2018); replace with water, unsweetened sparkling water, or other non-caloric beverages.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.94 g sugar per 100 g and GI of 63; liquid sugar in the absence of dietary fibre causes rapid postprandial glucose excursions
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid Coca-Cola entirely, as its high sugar content (9.94 g/100 g, predominantly from high fructose corn syrup) combined with zero dietary fibre and liquid delivery format produces rapid and pronounced postprandial blood glucose spikes; one 320 ml can delivers approximately 33 g of rapidly absorbed sugar with a glycemic load of approximately 20.9.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Ultra-processed sugar-sweetened beverage; regular consumption associated with increased triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and cardiovascular event risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of Coca-Cola, as habitual intake of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated serum triglycerides, increased visceral fat, and a higher risk of major adverse cardiovascular events (Malik et al., Circulation 2010); the beverage provides no cardioprotective nutrients and contributes only rapidly absorbed free sugars.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High fructose corn syrup promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; phosphoric acid may reduce calcium absorption and worsen bone density in patients on corticosteroids
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Coca-Cola, as high fructose corn syrup at 9.94 g sugar per 100 g promotes inflammatory cytokine production, and phosphoric acid in cola beverages may impair calcium absorption, compounding corticosteroid-induced bone loss; replace with anti-inflammatory beverages such as green tea.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola Original Taste (320 ml can) is a globally recognised carbonated cola soft drink sold in Singapore in a standard 320 ml aluminium can. It is an ultra-processed, ready-to-drink sugar-sweetened beverage formulated from carbonated water, high fructose corn syrup, caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. The beverage is dark brown, highly carbonated, and delivers a characteristic sweet-acidic cola flavour with a slight bitter finish from caffeine. It contains no alcohol, no protein of nutritional significance, and no dietary fibre. Per 100 g it provides 42 kcal, 10.36 g carbohydrate (of which 9.94 g is sugar), 0.25 g fat, and 3 mg sodium. One 320 ml can delivers approximately 134 kcal and 33 g of rapidly absorbed free sugars. The glycemic index is 63 (medium) and the glycemic load per can is approximately 20.9, making it a clinically significant source of glycemic stress. The product is halal-certified, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is contraindicated for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. Regular consumption is associated with increased risk of metabolic syndrome, dental caries, weight gain, and elevated triglycerides. It is widely available across Singapore convenience stores, supermarkets, and food-service outlets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Coke?
How much sugar is in a 320ml can of Coke?
Can I drink Coke if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of Coca-Cola?
How much caffeine in a can of Coke?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010030, confidence 0.95) for an exact product match. Saturated fat and trans fat confirmed zero by product formulation via label data. GI of 63 sourced directly from the University of Sydney GI Database and confirmed in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). All mandatory multilingual names present for Singapore-distributed product.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — Coca-Cola Original Taste contains no protein-contributing ingredients. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — the beverage contains no fibre-contributing ingredients. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero per product label data; consistent with product formulation containing no animal fats or tropical oils. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per product label data. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero by product formulation — no animal-derived ingredients. magnesium_mg is reported as 0.0 per HPB Foodo database (below detection threshold / trace). monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile not reported in HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific SKU as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo database for this product; Coca-Cola Original Taste is not a significant source of micronutrients and these values are not published in the primary approved source. Glycemic load per serving calculated as GI (63) × carbohydrate per 320 g serving (33.15 g) / 100 = 20.9. Portions in input contained three duplicate/near-duplicate entries; consolidated to one canonical serving of 320 g (1 can). The product is sold in Singapore and is widely distributed; halal status confirmed per cultural tags in input record.
